title: salix × kudoi
intro: Salix × kudoi, also known as Willow-Falsecottonwood or Japanese Hybrid Willow, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Salix koriyana and Salix sachalinensis. The medicinal properties of Salix × kudoi lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The plant's salicin content provides health benefits such as reducing inflammation and relieving pain, making it a potential treatment for conditions like arthritis and headaches. Salix × kudoi can be prepared into herbal remedies like tea, capsules, or salves, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
